content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance       Administration       Executive/Legal       Enforcement       Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662           322-5660            322-5901             322-6441          322-6444

April 11, 1980


Doris B. Black
City Clerk
401 East Chapman Avenue
Placentia, CA 92670

Dear Ms. Black:

    Your letter of March 18, 1980, has been referred to me
for reply. As I understand the matter, Mr. Bean filed his
initial campaign statement the day after it was due. From
the correspondence between Mr. Bean and yourself it appears
that he attempted to file the statement on the date that it
was due, but could not because your office was closed. Your
question concerns whether Government Code Section 91013
permits you to waive the fine it otherwise permits you to
impose for late filings.

    Government Code Section 91013(a) provides:

         If any person filed an original statement or report,
    after any deadline imposed by this act he shall, in
    addition to any other penalties or remedies established
    by this act, be liable in the amount of ten dollars
    ($10) per day after the deadline until the statement or
    report is filed, to the officer with whom the statement
    or report is required to be filed. Liability need not
    be enforced by the filing officer if on an impartial
    basis he determines that the late filing was not willful
    and that enforcement of the liability will not further
    the purposes of the act, except that no liability shall
    be waived if a statement or report is not filed within
    10 days, or five days in the case of the second preelection
    campaign statement, after the filing officer has sent
    specific written notice of the filing requirement.

    This section provides for waiver of fine if, in the exercise
of the filing officer's judgment, the late filing was not
willful and that imposition of a fine would not further the
purposes of the Act. The filing officer has no discretion
to waive a fine, however, if any person required to file has

Doris B. Black
April 11, 1980
Page Two


not filed within 10 days of his receiving written notice
that a deadline for filing has passed (in the case of the
first statement). Since Mr. Bean filed before any written
notice of his failure to file was received by him, your
discretion to waive the fine is intact. Under the Act,
therefore, you may waive the fine if you conclude that Mr. Bean's
failure to file was not willful and imposition of it will
not further the purposes of the Act.

I trust this answers your question. If I may be further
assistance to you, do not hesitate to call me.

Very truly yours,

Thomas M. Sobel
Chief
Legal Division

TMS:plh

.

The People Are The City

Mayor                                                                                           Councilmembers
WILLIAM E. RASHFORD                                                    VIRGINIA C. FARMER
                                                                                               NORMAN Z. ECKENRODE
City Administrator                                                           DONALD A. HOLT, JR.
EDWIN T. POWELL                                                          BETTY MEAD


401 East Chapman Avenue · Placentia, California 92670

March 18, 1980


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA  95184

Attention:  Enforcement Division

Gentlemen:

Enclosed for your information is the complete correspondence
involved in the recent late filing of Placentía City Council
candidate George R. Bean's first campaign statement. Please
note that Mr. Bean has paid the late filing penalty "under
protest".

After following advice from our City Attorney, as well as
advice received in telephone conversations with the Los Angeles
Fair Political Practices Commission office, the various corres-
pondence was mailed to the candidate. I feel that all actions
taken by this office were required to fulfill my responsibility
of filing officer.

Please review the matter and notify us as to whether the
actions taken were within the provisions of the Government
Code. If you have any questions, please contact myself or
Betty Wallis, Placentía Deputy City Clerk, (714) 993-8231.

Sincerely,

Doris B. Black

Doris B. Black
City Clerk

DBB:bw

Enclosures

Mayor
WILLIAM E. RASHFORD

City Administrator
EDWIN T. POWELL

The People Are The City

CITY OF
PLACENTIA

401 East Chapman Avenue · Placentia, California 92670

February 29, 1980

Mr. George R. Bean
120 Santa Rosa Way
Placentia, CA 92670

Dear Mr. Bean:

As you have previously been advised by correspondence dated
January 8, 1980, and correspondence dated February 22, 1980,
you were required to file a candidate's statement no later
than February 28, 1980, which covered campaign reporting
period ending February 25, 1980.

Under requirements of Government Code Section 91013, if any
person files a report or statement after any deadline, he shall
be liable in the amount of $10 per day after the deadline until
the statement or report is filed. You filed this report on
February 29, 1980.

The Code also provides as follows: "Liability need not be
enforced by the filing officer if on an impartial basis he
determines that the late filing was not willful and that enforce-
ment of the liability will not further the purpose of the act,
except that no liability shall be waived if a statement or report
is not filed within 10 days, or five days in the case of the
second preelection campaign statement, after the filing officer
has sent specific written notice of the filing requirement."

Please consider this specific written notice that you filed your
campaign statement one day late and have 10 days from date of
this letter to give reason why the penalty should be waived. If
there are facts which should support a waiver of the fine which
you feel the Clerk should consider, you should immediately supply
them in writing to the Clerk.

Councilmembers
VIRGINIA C. FARMER
NORMAN Z. ECKENRODE
DONALD A. HOLT, JR.
BETTY MEAD

CERTIFIED MAIL
NO. 177100

Mr. George R. Bean
February 29, 1980
Page Two

Until a determination is made regarding a waiver, you are liable
in the amount of $10 for the late campaign report. This amount
if due immediately and should be submitted to the City of
Placentia Finance Department. Should a waiver be determined
appropriate, this amount will be refunded to you.

Sincerely,

Doris B Black

Doris B. Black
City Clerk

2. ARTICLE ADDRESSED TO:

George R. Bean
120 Santa Rosa Way, Placentia

   DATE OF DELIVERY        [illegible]

5. ADDRESS (Complete only if requested)

[blank space]

6. UNABLE TO DELIVER BECAUSE:          CLERK'S
                                       INITIALS

ZCPO 1977-0-249-585

Mayor
WILLIAM E. RASHFORD

City Administrator
EDWIN T. POWELL

Councilmembers
VIRGINIA C. FARMER
NORMAN Z. ECKENRODE
DONALD A. HOLT, JR.
BETTY MEAD

The People Are The City

ALL AMERICA CITY

CITY OF
PLACENTIA

401 East Chapuian Avenue • Placentia, California 92670

March 10, 1980


Mr. George R. Bean
120 Santa Rosa Way
Placentia, CA 92670

Dear Mr. Bean:

Subject:  Payment of Fine for Late Filing of
          First Candidate Campaign Statement

A notice, dated February 29, 1980, was delivered to you by
certified mail advising you that you are liable in the amount
of $10.00 for the late filing of your first candidate's cam-
paign statement. It further advised that you have ten days in
which to submit a written request for waiver of the fine. We
did not receive a request for waiver from you within the time
permitted. (Government Code Section 91013)

Therefore, we have enclosed an invoice in the amount of $10.00
which represents the fine you are required to pay for filing
your campaign statement one day late.

You are hereby notified that, if we have not received payment
of this fine by 5:00 p.m., Monday, March 24, 1980, we are
required to forward the entire matter to the Orange County
District Attorney for enforcement. (Government Code Section
91001(b).

Your prompt attention to this matter will be appreciated.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Doris B. Black
City Clerk

DBB:bw

Enclosure

CITY OF PLACENTIA
401 E. Chapman Avenue
Placentia, California 92670

FINANCE DEPT. 714-993-8237
CITY CLERK (714) 993-8231

INVOICE                                                                                                                Invoice No. 80/201

George R. Bean
120 Santa Rosa Way
Placentia, CA 92670                                                                                    Invoice Date  March 12,


                                                                                                                                                               AMOUNT


Fine for filing first candidate's campaign statement
one day late (Government Code Section 91013)                                                    $10.00




Due and payable no later than March 24, 1980.




Please make check payable to City of Placentia. Direct all correspondence to
City Clerk, City of Placentia, 401 E. Chapman Ave., Placentia, CA 92670
RETURN ONE COPY OF INVOICE WITH YOUR REMITTANCE.

George R. Bean
Attorney at Law
112 No. Bradford
Placentia, California 92670                    (714) 524-0380


March 12, 1980


Doris B. Black
City Clerk
City of Placentia
401 East Chapman Ave
Placentia, CA 92670

Dear Mrs. Black:

     I have received your letter concerning the first filing
of the candidate's statement, which you have indicated
was filed a day late. It would appear that a waiver would
be in order since the purpose of the statute is to attempt
to force the filing of such statements which would otherwise
not be filed.

     In this particular instance the statement was due as you
stated on the 28th of February, and the statement was
executed and attempted to be delivered on said date, however,
by the time that I was able to get to the city hall it was
closed and I could only wait to turn it in the next day.
The day actually ends on the hour of 12:00 midnight, and it
would appear that if such is the law then there would have
to be accordations to permit the filing of such statements
up to and until such hour.

     The document was filed the following morning, within one
hour after your offices opened, at 9:00 a.m. It would seem
that there is no wilfulness on my part, and request that
this fee be waived. Especially in view of the fact that
no effort was required by any person other than to receive
the document.


                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        George R. Bean
GRB/rb

The People Are The City

Mayor                                                                                              Councilmembers
WILLIAM E. RASHFORD                                                           VIRGINIA C. FARMER
                                                                                                    NORMAN Z. ECKENRODE
City Administrator                                                              
EDWIN T. POWELL                                                                  DONALD A. HOLT, JR.
                                                                                                    BETTY MEAD


401 East Chapman Avenue · Placentia, California 92670                    (714) 993-8231

                                                                                March 14, 1980


Mr. George R. Bean
120 Santa Rosa Way
Placentia, CA 92670

Dear Mr. Bean:

We received this date your request for a waiver of the late filing
fee for your first candidate's campaign statement. However, as the
City Filing Officer, I am restricted under California Government
Code Section 91013 from waiving the liability because your request
was not received within the time provided for in this statute. This
Government Code Section was outlined in our notice to you sent
February 29, 1980.

Please remit the late filing fine to the City of Placentia prior to
5:00 p.m., March 24, 1980.

                                                                                Sincerely,

                                                                                Doris B. Black
                                                                                City Clerk

DBB:bw

George R. Bean
Attorney at Law
112 No. Bradford
Placentia, California 92670                                    (714) 524-0380

                                                               RECEIVED
                           March 14, 1980
                                                               CITY CLERK

                                                               MAR 17 1980
Doris B. Black, City Clerk
City of Placentia
401 East Chapman                                               CITY OF PLACENTIA
Placentia, CA 92670

                Re:  Imposition of Fine for Late Filing of
                     First Candidate Campaign Statement

Dear Mrs. Black:

     I am enclosing a check in the sum of $10.00 in response to
your request andstatement under invoice No. 80/201. This
is not an admission that there was a violation of C.C.C. 84200,
and is being submitted under protest, since the proper deter-
minations under C.C.C. 91013 have not been made.

     C.C.C. 91013 does not impose a time limit for the submission
of a request for waiver such as you have attempted to do, nor
does it impose a specific time within which any penalty is
required to have been paid. I had submitted to you a letter
requesting a waiver within the ten days after I had actually
received your letter. Further, no candidate is required to
submit a request for a waiver at all. It does however, require
that "on an impartial basis he determines that the late filing
was not wilful and that the enforcement of the liability will
not further the purposes of the act. . . ." This act on the
part of your office has not been done. ("he" meaning City Clerk).

     I can very well understand your desire to administer the duties
of your office, and would expect you to do so, however, it would
seem to me that you are going beyond the duty imposed under
the Political Reform Act. And, I might add, I don't believe
that you are acting in any manner other than out of a desire
to enforce the law as you understand it.

     I would have to point out to you that under the codes applicable,
I had until midnight of February 28, 1980 to file the statement
with your office. Your office was closed before the deadline.
Under section 81007, I could have mailed the statement and
would be deemed to have met the requirements, had it been
mailed prior to midnight. Instead, in order to have the
document in your hands at the earliest possible time, I waited
to the next morning, and promptly brought it to your office.

     The promptness on my part would show that there is no wilful
avoidance of the requirements.

                                         -1-

I have examined your letters with some care, and do not find
that your office has performed the following functions and
made the following determinations prior to demanding the penalty:

1. There has been no determination and finding
that on an "impartial basis" there has been a wilful late
filing.

2. There has been no determination and finding
that on an "impartial basis" that the imposition of the liability
would or would not further the purposes of the act.

I would submit to you that the purpose of the act is to make
certain that the necessary papers are filed, and not for the
purposes of creating a liability. This is further evidenced
by the fact that the total imposition of liability can be
no greater than $100.00, and that the "civil prosecutor" has
the responsibility for enforcement of liabilities, remedies
and sanctions. This particular liability is a civil matter
and is not within the scope of the district attorney as you
would suggest. This is not a "fine" under C.G.C. 91013.

In view of the above, it is my position that the necessary
prerequisites have not been met before you have demanded the
$10.00 liability, and therefore the $10.00 which you have
demanded is being submitted under protest.

Very truly yours,

George R. Bean

GRB/rb

-2-

CITY OF PLACENTIA
401 E. Chapman Avenue
Placentia, California 92670

FINANCE DEPT. 714-993-8237
CITY CLERK (714) 993-8231

INVOICE                                                                Invoice No. 80/201

George R. Bean
120 Santa Rosa Way
Placentia, CA 92670                                        Invoice Date  March 12,


                                                                          AMOUNT


Fine for filing first candidate's campaign statement
one day late (Government Code Section 91013)               $10.00

3/14/80  Payment is being made under protest that this is
not a "fine" within C.G.C. 91013, and secondly, the proper
determinations by the City Clerk have not been made in
conformance with the requirements of C.G.C. 91013.


Due and payable no later than March 24, 1980.


Please make check payable to City of Placentia. Direct all correspondence to
City Clerk, City of Placentia, 401 E. Chapman Ave., Placentia, CA 92670
RETURN ONE COPY OF INVOICE WITH YOUR REMITTANCE.

As I understand the matter, Mr. Bean filed his initial campaign statement the day after it was due. From the correspondence between Mr. Bean and yourself it appears that he attempted to file the statement on the date that it was due, but could not because your office was closed. Your question concerns whether Government Code Section 91013 permits you to waive the fine it otherwise permits you to impose for late filings. Government Code Section 91013(a) provides: If any person filed an original statement or report, after any deadline imposed by this act he shall, in addition to any other penalties or remedies established by this act, be liable in the amount of ten dollars ($10) per day after the deadline until the statement or report is filed, to the officer with whom the statement or report is required to be filed. Liability need not be enforced by the filing officer if on an impartial basis he determines that the late filing was not willful and that enforcement of the liability will not further the purposes of the act, except that no liability shall be waived if a statement or report is not filed within 10 days, or five days in the case of the second pre-election campaign statement, after the filing officer has sent specific written notice of the filing requirement. This section provides for waiver of fine if, in the exercise of the filing officer's judgment, the late filing was not willful and that imposition of a fine would not further the purposes of the Act. The filing officer has no discretion to waive a fine, however, if any person required to file has not filed within 10 days of his receiving written notice that a deadline for filing has passed (in the case of the first statement). Since Mr. Bean filed before any written notice of his failure to file was received by him, your discretion to waive the fine is intact. Under the Act, therefore, you may waive the fine if you conclude that Mr. Bean's failure to file was not willful and imposition of it will not further the purposes of the Act. I trust this answers your question. If I may be further assistance to you, do not hesitate to call me. Very truly yours, Thomas M.
